name: Arx Corinium
loadtext: The Second Empire made some advances into the periphery of Black Marsh, but they didn't stay long. Arx Corinium, once an Imperial fort, is now occupied by denizens of the deep swamp—and even stranger things.
summary: While previously constructed and held by the Imperials, the fort has fallen into disrepair and is overrun with Lamias. A group of nereids now occupy the flooded fort and focus on restoring balance to the land. Rumors say the fort is home to a great source of power, although it is perverse, corrupted. You can learn about its location from Mindil the Untested in Marbruk, Abzag the Monster in Shornhelm, or Maela the Crone in Stormhold. Its dungeon sets are Lamia's Song, Medusa, and Undaunted Infiltrator. There are always 4 treasure chests available to find in this dungeon, which may contain weapons, armor or jewelry from the dungeon's sets.
quest_note: Arx Corinium Objective: Explore the ruins of the Imperial fort Arx Corinium. / You helped the Nereid Sisters defeat the lamia nest.
normal_enemies: Giant Snakes; Ixniaa's Handmaidens; Lamias; Lamia Curares; Lamia Dominas; Menacing Lamias; Menacing Lamia Curares; Dead Lamia Divas; Dead Lamia Medicas
minibosses: Fanged Menace, a giant snake; Matron Ixniaa, a lamia matriarch; Ancient Lurcher, a lurcher
bosses: Ganakton the Tempest, a wamasu; Sliklenia the Songstress, one of the lamia matriarchs, and her pet; Sellistrix the Lamia Queen, the leader of the lamias
sets_note: Lamia's Song; Undaunted Infiltrator; Medusa; Sellistrix

name: The Banished Cells I
loadtext: The High Elves live long lives and have a talent for magic. Sometimes an Altmer's pursuit of magical power leads to tampering with forbidden knowledge and illicit dealings with evil Daedra. It was to confine such trespassers that the Banished Cells were created.
summary: In this version, Keeper Cirion needs your help binding the spirit of a dangerous Altmer, High Kinlord Rilis. You can learn about its location from Turuk Redclaws in Vulkhel Guard, Abzag the Monster in Daggerfall, or Roaring Ramavel in Davon's Watch. The final boss, High Kinlord Rilis, has a chance to drop the antiquity lead for the Pearls of Ehlnofey. See also: Banished Cells II
quest_note: The Banished Cells Objective: Explore the Banished Cells. / You braved the dangers of the Banished Cells.
normal_enemies: Banekin; Banished Archers, Mages, Royal Soldiers, Sentinels, Slayers and Soldiers; Clannfear; Scamps; Shadow Proxies
minibosses: Cell Haunter, a wraith; Angata the Clannfear Handler, a Dremora summoner; Skeletal Destroyer, a bone colossus
bosses: Shadowrend, a large clannfear; High Kinlord Rilis, the Altmer prisoner of the Banished Cells
sets_note: Sanctuary; Jailbreaker; Tormentor; Shadowrend (set)

name: The Banished Cells II
loadtext: The High Elves live long lives and have a talent for magic. Sometimes an Altmer's pursuit of magical power leads to tampering with forbidden knowledge and illicit dealings with evil Daedra. It was to confine such trespassers that the Banished Cells were created.
summary: The story started in Banished Cells I is continued in this version; High Kinlord Rilis has escaped again, and Keeper Cirion once again needs help banishing him for good. You can learn about its location from Turuk Redclaws in Vulkhel Guard, Abzag the Monster in Daggerfall, or Roaring Ramavel in Davon's Watch. Hazards include flame walls.
quest_note: The Banished Cells Objective: Explore the Banished Cells. / You braved the dangers of the Banished Cells.
normal_enemies: Banished Archers and Warriors; Dremora Clashers, Hauzkyn and Kyngald; Dark Banekin; Dark Clannfear; Dark Twilight; Daedroths; Flame Atronachs; Flesh Atronachs; Skeletal Warriors; Spider Daedra; Xivilai
minibosses: Keeper Areldur, a corrupted Keeper; Keeper Voranil, another corrupted Keeper; Sister Sihna and Sister Vera, a pair of harvesters
bosses: Maw of the Infernal, a giant daedroth; Keeper Imiril, another corrupted Keeper; High Kinlord Rilis, in his Xivilai form
sets_note: Sanctuary; Jailbreaker; Tormentor; Maw of the Infernal (set)

name: Blackheart Haven
loadtext: The Bjoulsae River, where it widens as it enters the Iliac Bay, flows past dozens of small islands that have long served as refuges for smugglers and sea raiders. The most notorious of these hideouts is Blackheart Haven.
summary: thumb|right|The island The island is the hideout of the infamous Captain Blackheart and his crew. A pirate, Shifty Tom, wants you to help him turn over a new leaf by defeating his former captain and crewmates. You can learn about its location from Tiny Talisse in Rawl'kha, Mighty Mordra in Evermore, or Ferocious Jakidi in Riften. Its final boss, Captain Blackheart, has a chance to drop the antiquity lead for the Torc of Tonal Constancy.
quest_note: Blackheart Haven Objective: Find the secrets of Blackheart Haven. / Captain Blackheart's reign of evil is over.
normal_enemies: Blackheart Bonelords; Blackheart Deckhands; Blackheart Frighteners; Blackheart Strongarms; Blackheart Summoners; Bone Scouts; Hags; Harpies; Haven Archers; Haven Bruisers; Haven Sinkers; Haven Slip-Knives; Sea Hounds; Undying Pillagers
minibosses: Iron-Heel, an Argonian pirate; First Mate Wavecutter, an Orc pirate; Hollow Heart, a wraith
bosses: Atarus, an ogrim that serves as the crew's "chef"; Roost Mother, a fire-breathing hagraven; Captain Blackheart, the skeleton leader of the pirates
sets_note: Undaunted Unweaver; Bone Pirate's Tatters; Knight-Errant's Mail; Pirate Skeleton

name: Blessed Crucible
loadtext: Warriors from all over Tamriel whisper about the Blessed Crucible, the hidden arena in the mountains of Skyrim where the mighty contend for fame, wealth, and the fabled Brimstone Crown.
summary: This dungeon takes place in a vast daedric ruin repurposed as a gladiatorial arena. In it, you must help Talres Voren pay off his debts by winning the tournament and becoming the Champion of the Crucible. You can learn about its location from Vlesyl the Skin-Stealer in Rawl'kha, Sluz the Beryl-Chested in Evermore, or Kailstig the Axe in Riften. Bottomless Pits and Lava Pools serve as environmental hazards in the dungeon. A letter is located on the top bunk of one of the beds to the right of the entrance to the Gladiator's Quarters. There is a chest to the left of this room's exit. Several books are located on the plaques of statues in the chamber preceding the Lava Queen's arena, which tell
quest_note: Blessed Crucible Objective: Test your mettle in the Blessed Crucible. / You are now Champion of the Blessed Crucible.
normal_enemies: Bears; Crucible Archers; Crucible Enforcers; Crucible Guardians; Crucible Durzogs; Enraged Durzogs; Gladiator Assassins; Gladiator Battlemages; Gladiator Brutes; Gladiator Champions; Gladiator Fearmages; Gladiator Frostmages; Gladiator Healers; Gladiator Hunters; Gladiator Necromancers; Gladiator Rangers; Gladiator Stalkers; Gladiator Summoners; Gladiator Warriors; Lava Atronach; Senche
minibosses: Grunt the Clever, a giant; Teranya the Faceless, a Crucible assassin; Captain Thoran, a Crucible mage time bomb mage
bosses: The Pack, four werewolves: Dynus Aralas, Kayd at-Sal, Nusana and Snagg gro-Mashul; The Beast Master and her creatures:; Incineration Beetles, four magically corrupted shalks; Stinger, a massive giant scorpion; The Troll King, a massive armored troll; The Lava Queen, the current Champion of the Crucible
sets_note: Nikulas' Heavy Armor; Noble Duelist's Silks; Sword Dancer; The Troll King (set)

name: City of Ash I
loadtext: Not all Wood Elves are happy about Valenwood joining the Aldmeri Dominion. Some fear for their beloved forests, while others fear their heritage and traditions will be diluted or replaced. A few groups have retreated to refuges deep in the jungle.
summary: thumb|right|City of Ash I Formerly known as the graht-oak city Whisper Grove, a recently opened Oblivion Gate and hordes of Daedra have turned the Bosmeri city into a dangerous burning ruin. The city's desperate inhabitants have even resorted to breaking the Green Pact, constructing barriers of fallen wood to stem the flow of invading Daedra. You can learn about its location from Mindil the Untested in Marbruk, Abzag the Monster in Shornhelm, or Roaring Ramavel in Stormhold. The story continues in City of Ash II.
quest_note: City of Ash Objective: The City of Ash is under attack. / You helped the Bosmer repel the Daedric invasion.
normal_enemies: An Acolyte, Assassin and Initiate Exemplar; Banekin and Banekin Minions; Dremora Hauzkyn and Kynvals; A Dremora Berserker and Shaman; Dagonite Archers, Assassins and Warriors; Flame Atronachs; Imps; Scamps
minibosses: Golor the Banekin Handler, a Bosmer cultist; Dark Ember, a large flame atronach; Rothariel Flameheart, a Bosmer cultist
bosses: Infernal Guardian, a flaming lurcher; Warden of the Shrine, a dremora nightblade; Razor Master Erthas, the Bosmer leader of the Dagonite cult
sets_note: Burning Spellweave; Sunderflame; Embershield; Infernal Guardian (set)

name: City of Ash II
loadtext: Nature has returned to reclaim the City of Ash. An unsealed portal still threatens, and only a few remain to defend against it.
summary: thumb|right|City of Ash II The storyline continues from the events of City of Ash I. In this version, Fingaenion intends to travel to the Deadlands to rescue his wife Gilraen from Mehrunes Dagon, and he needs your help. You can learn about its location from Mindil the Untested in Marbruk, Abzag the Monster in Shornhelm, or Roaring Ramavel in Stormhold. Centipedes and scorpions are found in numbers inside. Hazards include bottomless pits and lava pools.
quest_note: City of Ash Objective: The City of Ash is under attack. / You helped the Bosmer repel the Daedric invasion.
normal_enemies: Clannfear; Crocodile; Dremora Beldakyn; Dremora Gandrakyn; Dremora Harstryl; Dremora Hauzkyn; Dremora Kyngald; Dremora Kynlurker; Dremora Kynval; Dremora Morikyn; Fire Ravager; Flame Atronach; Scamp; Senche-Tiger; Storm Atronach; Strangler; Urata Elementalist; Urata Militant; Venomous Skeleton; Xivilai Immolator; Xivilai Ravager
minibosses: Rukhan, a xivilai warrior and his two dremora assistants Akezel and Marruz; Urata the Legion, a Dremora fire mage; Xivilai Boltaic, one of two guardians blocking the bridge to Skoria; Xivilai Fulminator, one of two guardians blocking the bridge to Skoria
bosses: Horvantud the Fire Maw, a large armored daedroth; Ash Titan, a daedric titan; Valkyn Skoria, a high-ranking dremora clad in molten armor
sets_note: Burning Spellweave; Sunderflame; Embershield; Valkyn Skoria (set)

name: Crypt of Hearts I
loadtext: This place's forbidding black towers are visible for leagues, even through Rivenspire's drifting mists. Yet the local inhabitants never mention it, behaving as if it doesn't even exist. But the Crypt of Hearts is no illusion or mirage—it is solid and all too real.
summary: Formerly a school teaching Destruction magic, sword fighting, alchemy, and possibly even Daedric summoning, the crypt is now home to some of the tortured souls of its teachers and of its students. In this version Alanwe needs you to piece together her soul which is currently contained in three phylacteries scattered throughout the Crypt. You can learn about its location from Eraegaer the Kill-Happy in Marbruk, Ilmindil the Incendiary in Shornhelm, or Roaring Ramavel in Stormhold. The final bosses, Ilambris-Athor and Ilambris-Zaven, have a chance to drop the antiquity lead for the Bloodlord's Embrace. See also: Crypt of Hearts II
quest_note: Crypt of Hearts Objective: Explore the Crypt of Hearts. / You braved the dangers of the Crypt of Hearts.
normal_enemies: Banekin; Ghosts; The Mage Master's Slaves; Skeletal Archers; Skeletal Pyromancers; Skeletal Runecasters; Skeletal Warriors; Wraiths; Zombie; Zombie Plague-bearers; Zombie Stalkers
minibosses: The Mage Master, a skeleton nullifier; Uulkar Bonehand, a lich; Dogas the Berserker, a Dremora voidstalker
bosses: Archmaster Siniel, an Altmer necromancer mummy; Death's Leviathan, a giant bone colossus; Ilambris-Athor and Ilambris-Zaven, a pair of Xivilai torturers
sets_note: Shroud of the Lich; Leviathan; Ebon Armory; Ilambris

name: Crypt of Hearts II
loadtext: This place's forbidding black towers are visible for leagues, even through Rivenspire's drifting mists. Yet the local inhabitants never mention it, behaving as if it doesn't even exist. But the Crypt of Hearts is no illusion or mirage—it is solid and all too real.
summary: Formerly a school teaching Destruction magic, sword fighting, alchemy, and possibly even Daedric summoning, the crypt is now home to some of the tortured souls of its teachers and of its students. The storyline continues from the events of Crypt of Hearts I. In this version, Alanwe once again needs your help defeating her former husband, who has returned to torment her following her escape from torture. You can learn about its location from Eraegaer the Kill-Happy in Marbruk, Ilmindil the Incendiary in Shornhelm, or Roaring Ramavel in Stormhold. The final boss, Nerien'eth, has a chance to drop the antiquity lead for the Pearls of Ehlnofey. See also: Crypt of Hearts I
quest_note: Crypt of Hearts Objective: Explore the Crypt of Hearts. / You braved the dangers of the Crypt of Hearts.
normal_enemies: Death Spiders; Flesh Atronachs; Ibelgast's Broodnurses; Ibelgast's Cauterizers; Ibelgast's Flesh Atronachs; Ibelgast's Wefters; Skeletons; Spider; Spiderkith Broodnurses; Spiderkith Cauterizers; Spiderkith Enervators; Spiderkith Venifexes; Spiderkith Warpers; Spiderkith Wefters; Wraith
minibosses: Ibelgast, a spiderkith nullifier; Chamber Guardian, a skeleton ravager; Mezeluth, a spiderkith fire mage
bosses: Ruzozuzalpamaz, a spider daedra; The Ilambris Amalgam, a large bone colossus formed from the fused bodies of Ilambris-Athor and Ilambris-Zaven; Nerien'eth, Alanwe's former husband who went insane after wielding the Ebony Blade
sets_note: Shroud of the Lich; Leviathan; Ebon Armory; Nerien'eth (set)

name: Darkshade Caverns I
loadtext: These caves have the distinction of being the most famous kwama mines in Deshaan. Dark Elves consider kwama eggs a delicacy, and those harvested from Darkshade Caverns are particularly prized by Dunmeri gourmets.
summary: thumb|right|Inside the cavernsthumb|right|The ruins of Rkugamz As Deshaan's largest kwama mine, the caverns' ownership remains hotly contested. The caverns also contain the hidden Dwemer ruin of Rkugamz. In the normal version, Tervur Sadri wants you to help him reclaim his mine from the thugs within. You can learn about its location from Mad Zebba outside Elden Root, Lady Blade outside Wayrest, or Hranir Icy-Mane outside Mournhold. The following item sets will drop both Darkshade Caverns I and II: Armor of Truth, Netch's Touch, and Strength of the Automaton.
quest_note: Darkshade Caverns Objective: Explore the Darkshade Caverns. / You braved the dangers of the Darkshade Caverns.
normal_enemies: Betty Netch; Bull Netch; Centurion Spiders; Darkshade Alits; Dwarven Spiders; Dwarven Spheres; Kwama Scribs; Kwama Warriors; Kwama Workers; Pit Rats Enforcers; Pit Rats Menders; Pit Rats Rangers; Pit Rats Thugs; Taskmaster Anaba; Taskmaster Elitai; Taskmaster Ninbael; Strangler Vines
minibosses: Head Shepherd Neloren, a Dunmer member of the Pit Rats; Cavern Patriarch, a large netch; Cutting Sphere, a Dwemer Sphere
bosses: Foreman Llothan, the leader of the Pit Rats; The Hive Lord, a large Kwama Warrior; Sentinel of Rkugamz, a Dwarven Centurion blocking the entrance to Rkugamz
sets_note: Netch's Touch; Strength of the Automaton; Armor of Truth; Sentinel of Rkugamz (set)

name: Darkshade Caverns II
loadtext: These caves have the distinction of being the most famous kwama mines in Deshaan. Dark Elves consider kwama eggs a delicacy, and those harvested from Darkshade Caverns are particularly prized by Dunmeri gourmets.
summary: thumb|right|Inside the cavernsthumb|right|The ruins of Rkugamz As Deshaan's largest kwama mine, the caverns' ownership remains hotly contested. The caverns also contain the hidden Dwemer ruin of Rkugamz. The storyline continues from the event of Darkshade Caverns I. In this version, a Mages Guild researcher named Shatasha needs your help destroying a dangerous Dwemer mechanism within Rkugamz, before it corrupts Deshaan above. You can learn about its location from Mad Zebba outside Elden Root, Lady Blade outside Wayrest, or Hranir Icy-Mane outside Mournhold. Its dungeon sets are Armor of Truth, Netch's Touch, and Strength of the Automaton. Darkshade Caverns II is accessible starting at level
quest_note: Darkshade Caverns Objective: Explore the Darkshade Caverns. / You braved the dangers of the Darkshade Caverns.
normal_enemies: Dwarven Sphere; Dwarven Spider; Fallen Marksman; Fallen Miner; Kwama Scrib; Kwama Warrior; Kwama Worker; Netchling; Transmuted Marksman; Transmuted Miner; Transmuted Scrib; Transmuted Warrior; Transmuted Worker

bosses: Transmuted Hive Lord, an unusually large kwama warrior along with his draining and sedating scribs.; Grobull the Transmuted, a large netch; The Engine Guardian, a giant Dwemer spider guarding the Dwemer mechanism
sets_note: Netch's Touch; Strength of the Automaton; Armor of Truth; Engine Guardian

name: Direfrost Keep
loadtext: Ancestral home of the Direfrost clan, this frozen castle has long been sealed to the outside world, its owners neither seen nor heard from in generations. Mountain storms rage above the castle walls, conjured up by some unknown malignant power.
summary: Once the ancestral home of the witch hunting Direfrost clan, the castle has been abandoned following an attack by the ice witch Drodda. A surviving member of the clan, Eboric Direfrost, needs your help to reclaim his home. You can learn about its location from Untouchable Naim outside Velyn Harbor, Twice-Lucky in Sentinel, or Felvyn the Swordarm in Windhelm. Its dungeon sets are Draugr Hulk, Magicka Furnace, and The Ice Furnace. A chest can spawn under the bridge leading to the keep's main gate, and on top of the sewer grate that leads into the dungeon. Its final boss, Drodda of Icereach, has a chance to drop the antiquity lead for the Snow Treaders.
quest_note: Direfrost Keep Objective: Explore the Direfrost Keep. / You defeated Drodda and freed Direfrost Keep from her grasp.
normal_enemies: Bears; Direfrost Cryomancers; Direfrost Stormmasters; Draugr Archers; Draugr Stormlords; Draugr Warriors; Frostkin; Frost Atronachs; Frozen Soldiers; Ice Wraiths; Garmath the Bold; Lord Agomar; Orva Direfrost
minibosses: Teethnasher the Frostbound, a large frost troll; Drodda's Dreadlord, a dangerous draugr; Drodda's Apprentice, a powerful ice mage
bosses: Guardian of the Flame, a draugr; Iceheart, a large frost atronach; Drodda of Icereach, the witch responsible for the destruction of Direfrost Keep
sets_note: Magicka Furnace; Draugr Hulk; Ice Furnace; Iceheart (set)

name: Elden Hollow I
loadtext: When the Daedra-worshiping Ayleids were driven from Cyrodiil after Alessia's Slave Rebellion, many of the Wild Elf clans took refuge in Valenwood. One of the foremost of those clans was granted the right to excavate dwelling-halls under Elden Root itself.
summary: In this version, the Mages Guild is looking into reports of a strange ritual happening within Elden Hollow and need protection from the hostile Wood Orcs of the Darkfern Clan. You can learn about its location from Venomous Fauridil outside Elden Root, Hel Ra'Lala outside Wayrest, or Thongar the Stout outside Mournhold. Its final boss, Canonreeve Oraneth, has a chance to drop the antiquity lead for the Ring of the Wild Hunt.
quest_note: Elden Hollow Objective: Explore Elden Hollow. / You braved the dangers of the Elden Hollow.
normal_enemies: Darkfern Archers, Diggers, Flamerenders, Healers, Maulers, Skeletons and Stalkers; Justiciar Elanar; Stranglers and Strangler Saplings; Spriggans; Thalmor Adepts and Justiciars
minibosses: Ancient Spriggan, a spriggan; Nenesh gro-Mal, a Darkfern Orc warrior; Leafseether, a giant alit
bosses: Akash gra-Mal, a Darkfern Orc warrior; Chokethorn, a giant strangler; Canonreeve Oraneth, a Thalmor official attempting a Daedric summoning ritual
sets_note: Light Speaker; Barkskin; Undaunted Bastion; Chokethorn (set)

name: Elden Hollow II
loadtext: When the Daedra-worshiping Ayleids were driven from Cyrodiil after Alessia's Slave Rebellion, many of the Wild Elf clans took refuge in Valenwood. One of the foremost of those clans was granted the right to excavate dwelling-halls under Elden Root itself.
summary: The story continues from the events of Elden Hollow I. In this version, Kargand needs your help rescuing his sister Bakkhara from a Daedric realm. She has been trapped there because of the ritual performed by Canonreeve Oraneth earlier. You can learn about its location from Venomous Fauridil outside Elden Root, Hel Ra'Lala outside Wayrest, or Thongar the Stout outside Mournhold.
quest_note: Elden Hollow Objective: Explore Elden Hollow. / You braved the dangers of the Elden Hollow.
normal_enemies: Banekin; Dremora Fearkyn, Invokers and Kynvals; Frenzied and Mystic Guardians; Infested Banekin, Fearkyn and Invokers; Scamps; Nova Tendrils (Hard Mode only); Shadow Tendrils
minibosses: Dubroze the Infestor, a large daedroth; Azara the Frightener, a winged twilight; The Shadow Guard, a dremora shaman
bosses: Dark Root, a shadowy spriggan; Murklight, a shadowy lurcher; Bogdan the Nightflame, the daedric titan lord of the pocket plane seeping into the hollow
sets_note: Light Speaker; Barkskin; Undaunted Bastion; Nightflame

name: Fungal Grotto I
loadtext: These extensive caverns in the volcanic rock beneath Stonefalls were once used for unknown purposes by the ancient Chimer, but were abandoned by the Elves long ago. Now they are the haunt of Goblins and other denizens of the underworld.
summary: thumb|right|Inside the Grotto The cave is infested with goblins and dreugh, though the goblins are scared of something dark lurking deep within the grotto. In this version, Vila Theran wants you to clear the path to a shrine hidden within the grotto. You can learn about its location from Mindil the Untested in Vulkhel Guard, Abzag the Monster in Daggerfall, or Kailstig the Axe in Davon's Watch. Its dungeon sets are Dreugh King Slayer, Spider Cultist Cowl, and Viper's Sting.
quest_note: Fungal Grotto Objective: Explore the Fungal Grotto. / You braved the dangers of Fungal Grotto.

minibosses: Tazkad the Packmaster, a goblin hunter; Broodbirther, a large dreugh; Clatterclaw, a giant mudcrab
bosses: War Chief Ozozai, the leader of the goblins; Kra'gh the Dreugh King, leader of the dreugh

name: Fungal Grotto II
loadtext: These extensive caverns in the volcanic rock beneath Stonefalls were once used for unknown purposes by the ancient Chimer, but were abandoned by the Elves long ago. Now they are the haunt of Goblins and other denizens of the underworld.
summary: The storyline continues from the events of Fungal Grotto I. In this version, Defender Two-Blades needs you to defeat the Spider Cultists who have taken control of a dangerous artifact called the Obsidian Husk. You can learn about its location from Mindil the Untested in Vulkhel Guard, Abzag the Monster in Daggerfall, or Kailstig the Axe in Davon's Watch. Its dungeon sets are Dreugh King Slayer, Spider Cultist Cowl, and Viper's Sting.
quest_note: Fungal Grotto Objective: Explore the Fungal Grotto. / You braved the dangers of Fungal Grotto.

name: Selene's Web
loadtext: Even by the standards of , something is not right about the growth of the forest in this vicinity. It doesn't seem unhealthy—on the contrary, it seems vigorous, but in an unwholesome way.
summary: A group of Mages Guild members led by Warlock Carindon are exploring Selene's Web in search of a mysterious forest spirit, and need protection from hostile natives and wildlife. You can learn about its location from Thulga, Empress of Bone in Rawl'kha, Slays-By-Moon in Evermore, or Kailstig the Axe in Riften. Hazards include bear traps and bottomless pits.
quest_note: Selene's Web Objective: Explore Selene's Web. / You helped the Mages Guild defeat Selene.
normal_enemies: Earth Menders, Root Guards and True Shots; Hoarvors; Selene's Daggers, Rangers, Roses, Savages, Shamans, and Thundermauls; Senche Spirits; Spiders; Wolves

bosses: Longclaw, a Khajiit hunter and his senche daughters (Heartstalker, Nighteyes, Shadowhiskers, and Silentpaw); Foulhide, a giant bear; Selene, a Bosmer changeling
sets_note: Vestments of the Warlock; Hircine's Veneer; Durok's Bane; Selene (set)

name: Spindleclutch I
loadtext: The Ayleid ruins of Dwynnarth that grin down from the bluffs above Hag Fen were plundered and emptied out generations ago. But it seems there are forgotten mine tunnels beneath the lowest levels of Dwynnarth that might not be quite so empty.
summary: The cave is infested with spiders, although something more sinister lurks within. Praxin Douare wants your help clearing the cave of spiders and other possible threats. In Spindleclutch I, a constant, pervasive whispering sound is present throughout the dungeon, and only gets louder as you descend through the caves. You can learn about its location from Mindil the Untested in Vulkhel Guard, Mighty Mordra in Daggerfall, or Roaring Ramavel in Davon's Watch. Its dungeon sets are Knightmare, Prayer Shawl, and Spelunker. Its final boss, The Whisperer, has a chance to drop the antiquity lead for the Bloodlord's Embrace.
quest_note: Spindleclutch Objective: Explore the Spindleclutch caverns. / You braved the dangers within the Spindleclutch caverns.
normal_enemies: Corrupted Evoker; Corrupted Healer; Corrupted Guardian; Corrupted Infantry; Corrupted Knight; Spiders; Spider Hatchling; Spider Swarm
minibosses: Spindlekin, a giant spider; Cerise the Widow-Maker, a corrupted Breton berserker; Big Rabbu, a corrupted Redguard brute
bosses: Swarm Mother, a spider queen responsible for the spider infestation; The Whisperer, a spider daedra responsible for driving the Fighters Guild members insane
sets_note: Prayer Shawl; Spelunker; Knightmare; Swarm Mother (set)

name: Spindleclutch II
loadtext: The Ayleid ruins of Dwynnarth that grin down from the bluffs above Hag Fen were plundered and emptied out generations ago. But it seems there are forgotten mine tunnels beneath the lowest levels of Dwynnarth that might not be quite so empty.
summary: thumb|right|Entrance to Vorenor's lairthumb|right|The lair after Vorenor has been defeatedThe storyline continues from the events of Spindleclutch I. In this version, Guardian Sud-Hareem needs help finding his son, who he believes has been captured by vampires within Spindleclutch. You can learn about its location from Mindil the Untested in Vulkhel Guard, Mighty Mordra in Daggerfall, or Roaring Ramavel in Davon's Watch. Its dungeon sets are Knightmare, Prayer Shawl, and Spelunker.
quest_note: Spindleclutch Objective: Explore the Spindleclutch caverns. / You braved the dangers within the Spindleclutch caverns.
normal_enemies: Bloodfiends; Boneman Archers; Boneman Warriors; Corrupted Evoker Nightmares; Corrupted Healer Nightmares; Corrupted Infantry Nightmares; Spider Swarms; Thralled Archers; Thralled Brutes; Thralled Strikers; Thralled Warriors; Vampire Bonelords; Vampire Illusionists; Vampire Necromancers; Vampire Nightblades
elite_enemies: Big Rabbu Nightmare; Flesh Atronachs; Swarm Mother Nightmare; The Whisperer Nightmare; The Widow-Maker Nightmare
minibosses: Mad Mortine, a bloodfiend; Flesh Atronach Trio, a group of three powerful flesh atronachs who must be defeated together; Urvan Veleth, a Dunmer vampire
bosses: Bloodspawn, a gargoyle; Praxin Douare, the wraith of a former Fighters Guild member; Vorenor Winterbourne, a Nord vampire lord
sets_note: Prayer Shawl; Spelunker; Knightmare; Bloodspawn (set)

name: Tempest Island
loadtext: Surrounded by sand bars and shifting channels, this island in the Strid estuary has long been a haven for pirates, raiders, and fugitives from law enforcement. It has never been fully explored by any civilized authority.
summary: It has been invaded by the Sea Vipers, who use it to hone their storm magic for a planned assault on the Valenwood coast. A group of Dominion soldiers led by Battlereeve Alduril needs your help defeating the Maormer invaders and their lamia allies. You can learn about its location from Turuk Redclaws outside Velyn Harbor, Vyric the White in Sentinel, or Strong-With-Tail in Windhelm.
quest_note: Tempest Island Objective: Join the expedition to conquer Tempest Island. / You have defeated the Maormer Stormreeve Neidir.
normal_enemies: Lamias; Lamia Curares; Lamia Dominas; Lightning Avatars; Maormer Summoners; Sea Viper Berserkers; Sea Viper Chargers; Sea Viper Deadeyes; Sea Viper Healer; Sea Viper Strongarms
elite_enemies: Storm Atronachs (Normal rank during Stormfist fight)
minibosses: Sonolia the Matriarch, the leader of the lamia on Tempest Island; Yalorasse the Speaker, a Sea Viper rogue; Commodore Ohmanil, a Sea Viper commander
bosses: Valaran Stormcaller, a Sea Viper spellsword; Stormfist, a massive storm atronach; Stormreeve Neidir, the leader of the Sea Vipers
sets_note: Overwhelming Surge; Storm Master; Jolting Arms; Stormfist (set)

name: Vaults of Madness
loadtext: The Vaults of Madness are the masterpiece of the infamous Mad Architect, who is said to be one of the few entities ever to serve multiple Daedric Princes and profit from it. How he is paid for his "services" is a matter of wild and horrible conjecture.
summary: Gasteau Chamrond needs you to help him and his friends escape from the Vaults by destroying the Mad Architect. You can learn about its location from Tubidan the Alarmist in the Hollow City. Hazards include bottomless pits and fire, the latter of which is encountered near the Ancient One and Iskra the Omen. thumb|right|Interior
quest_note: Vaults of Madness Objective: Explore the Vaults of Madness. / You defeated the Mad Architect.
normal_enemies: Banekin; Daedroth; Dremora Bonelords, Fearkyn and Mages; Feral Shriven; Flame Atronachs; Flame Bringers; Ogrim; Reanimated Atronachs, Mages and Monstrosities; Seducer Archers, Brutes, Necromancers and Nightblades; Skeletal Healers and Torturers; Zombies
minibosses: The Cursed One, a wraith; Death's Head, a large bone colossus; Achaeraizur, a large daedroth; The Ancient One, a large watcher
bosses: Ulguna Soul-Reaver, Gasteau's harvester tormentor; Grothdarr, a massive flesh atronach; Iskra the Omen, a daedric titan; Mad Architect, a lich and designer of the Vaults
sets_note: The Worm's Raiment; Oblivion's Edge; Rattlecage; Grothdarr (set)

name: Volenfell
loadtext: The ancient Dwarven city of Volenfell was long lost to the sands of the Alik'r Desert. But its ruins have recently been discovered, prompting a race between scholars and treasure-hunters to be the first to search its storied depths.
summary: The researcher Tharayya wants your help finding a Dwemer artifact called the Guardian's Eye. You can learn about its location from Earrona Kneecapper outside Velyn Harbor, Magnifico Jalid in Sentinel, or Felvyn the Swordarm in Windhelm. thumb|right|The interior of Volenfell
quest_note: Volenfell Objective: Explore the ruins of Volenfell. / You've escaped the ruins of Volenfell.
normal_enemies: Assassin Beetles; Boilbite's Assassin Beetles; Desert Lionesses; Dwarven Centurions; Dwarven Spheres; Dwarven Spiders; Imperial Overseers; Lions; Lionesses; Treasure Hunters; Treasure Hunter Healers; Treasure Hunter Incendiaries; Unstable Dwarven Spiders; Quest-only; Mummies; Skeletons
minibosses: Desert Lion, a white lion; Boilbite, a large shalk; Unstable Construct, a charged dwemer sphere
bosses: Quintus Verres, Tharayya's traitorous husband, who awakens the Monstrous Gargoyle; Tremorscale, a large duneripper; The Guardian Council, a group of three dwemer centurions: The Guardian's Spark, The Guardian's Soul and The Guardian's Strength
sets_note: Treasure Hunter; Crusader; Duneripper's Scales; Tremorscale (set)

name: Wayrest Sewers I
loadtext: Wayrest is justifiably proud of the commodious modern sewers that drain the city's wastes into the Iliac Bay. But the curving conduits beneath the streets contain dark secrets unsuspected by those who walk in the light of day above.
summary: When you enter the sewers in the normal version, Investigator Irnand will request that you find and kill a rogue necromancer and his conspirators before they take over Wayrest above. You can learn its location from Wandering Jakalor outside Elden Root, Lady Blade outside Wayrest, or Hranir Icy-Mane outside Mournhold.
quest_note: Wayrest Sewers Objective: Explore Wayrest Sewers. / You braved the dangers of the Wayrest Sewers.
normal_enemies: Fiendish Hallucination; Mudcrabs; Pellingare Bowman; Pellingare Cutthroat; Pellingare Henchman; Pellingare Necromancer; Pellingare Recruit; Pellingare Sawbones; Restless Souls; Skeevers; Vermin Feeder
minibosses: Slimecraw, a giant crocodile; The Rat Whisperer, an eccentric man with control over skeevers; Uulgarg the Hungry, an Orc mercenary
bosses: Investigator Garron, a former city investigator-turned necromancer who has turned on his comrades; Varaine Pellingare, a nobleman who is plotting to take over Wayrest; Allene Pellingare, a noblewoman who is plotting to take over Wayrest
sets_note: Combat Physician; Toothrow; Sergeant's Mail; Slimecraw (set)

name: Wayrest Sewers II
loadtext: Wayrest is justifiably proud of the commodious modern sewers that drain the city's wastes into the Iliac Bay. But the curving conduits beneath the streets contain dark secrets unsuspected by those who walk in the light of day above.
summary: The storyline continues from the events of Wayrest Sewers I. In this version, enemies you once thought dead have returned. Yahyif and Gedric Serre want you to find Master Pellingare, who has gone missing in the city sewers. You can learn its location from Wandering Jakalor outside Elden Root, Lady Blade outside Wayrest, or Hranir Icy-Mane outside Mournhold.
quest_note: Wayrest Sewers Objective: Explore Wayrest Sewers. / You braved the dangers of the Wayrest Sewers.
normal_enemies: Bone Flayers; Escaped Souls; Fiendish Hallucinations; Fiendish Nightmares; Ghosts; Hired Necromancers; Hired Spellbinders; Skeletal Cutthroats; Skeletal Pyromancers; Skeletal Warriors; Zombies; Zombie Harbingers
minibosses: Skull Reaper, a large bone colossus; Uulgarg the Risen, the reanimated skeleton of Uulgarg the Hungry; The Forgotten One, a ghost
bosses: Malubeth the Scourger, a harvester summoned by the Pellingares; Garron the Returned, the lich form of Investigator Garron, who raised himself after death; Allene Pellingare and Varaine Pellingare, in their undead forms
sets_note: Combat Physician; Toothrow; Sergeant's Mail; Scourge Harvester
